mission.title: kube-ovn: [BUG] OVN EIP, FIP, SNAT mode. Packet loss and asymmetric return traffic on Hetzner vSwitch
mission.description: [BUG] OVN EIP, FIP, SNAT mode. Packet loss and asymmetric return traffic on Hetzner vSwitch. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kube-ovn troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your Kube-OVN deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
kubectl logs -l app=ovs -n kube-system --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to packet loss or asymmetric routing in OVN EIP/FIP/SNAT mode.

steps.title: Review kube-ovn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the Kube-OVN configuration and OVN resources:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=kube-ovn
kubectl get ovn-eip -A
kubectl get ovn-fip -A
kubectl get ovn-snat-rule -A
```
This issue manifests as intermittent packet loss and asymmetric return traffic when using Kube-OVN in OVN EIP, FIP, SNAT mode on multi-node clusters, particularly on Hetzner vSwitch infrastructure.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] OVN EIP, FIP, SNAT mode. Packet loss and asymmetric…
steps.description:
The root cause involves asymmetric routing in multi-node clusters with distributed gateway enabled. The ICMP request leaves via one node but the reply returns via a different node.

Diagnostic steps:
```bash
# Check OVN EIP assignments
kubectl get oeip -A

# Capture traffic on external interfaces to verify routing symmetry
kubectl -n kube-system exec -it <ovs-pod> -- tcpdump -i br-external -n icmp

# Verify OVN logical router policies
kubectl -n kube-system exec -it <ovn-central-pod> -- ovn-nbctl lr-policy-list ovn-cluster
```

See the source issue for community discussion: https://github.com/kubeovn/kube-ovn/issues/5280

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] OVN EIP, FIP, SNAT mode. Packet loss and… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that traffic is no longer asymmetric:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=ovs -n kube-system --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kube-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep -i ovn | tail -10

# Test connectivity from an EIP-enabled pod
kubectl exec -it <pod> -- ping -c 5 8.8.8.8
```
Confirm that packet loss and asymmetric return traffic issues are resolved.

resolution.summary:
Based on your detailed description and the information available from the Kube-OVN codebase and changelogs, here are some focused suggestions and observations to help diagnose and narrow down the cause of the packet loss and asymmetric return traffic in your multi-node setup:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-ovn installed or the issue environment reproducible.
